source.description: A six-panel pencil-and-colored-pencil process contact sheet reviewed as one cumulative still-life drawing.
source.notes: One squat faceted ink bottle sits at lower left beside one uncapped fountain pen angled upward on warm off-white paper. All bottle, nib, barrel, ink, highlight, and shadow features appear before the finishing panel.

process_strategy: The sequence fixes the bottle block and diagonal pen axis together, develops the glass silhouette and pen taper, adds the bottle panel and nib landmarks, introduces glass planes and small reflections, then layers restrained blue and brown pencil before final graphite cleanup only.

final_elements.name: bottle block, neck ellipse, and diagonal pen axis
final_elements.description: A light squat bottle envelope with a centered neck ellipse and one long pen centerline rising to the right.

final_elements.name: faceted bottle contour and fountain-pen silhouette
final_elements.description: Sloped glass shoulders, a short threaded neck, beveled lower corners, and a tapered pen barrel ending in an exposed nib.

final_elements.name: front glass panel, ink level, grip band, and nib details
final_elements.description: A blank inset front panel and curved ink line on the bottle plus a small grip band, nib slit, and breathing hole on the pen.

final_elements.name: glass edge lines and reserved highlight shapes
final_elements.description: A few inner glass edges and pale reflection shapes that clarify the already established bottle planes without adding a label or lettering.

final_elements.name: blue-black ink, warm brown barrel, metal nib tone, and cast shadow
final_elements.description: Restrained colored pencil fills the existing ink and barrel shapes, lightly warms the nib, and grounds both objects with one soft shadow.

frames.visible_job: Blocks a squat bottle, centered neck ellipse, and one long diagonal pen axis at a fixed scale.
introduces: bottle block, neck ellipse, and diagonal pen axis

frames.visible_job: Builds the bottle shoulders, neck, beveled base, tapered pen barrel, grip, and exposed nib around the construction.
introduces: faceted bottle contour and fountain-pen silhouette
requires_prior_elements: bottle block, neck ellipse, and diagonal pen axis

frames.step_name: Add the ink and nib landmarks
frames.visible_job: Adds the bottle's blank front panel and curved ink line, then places the pen grip band, nib slit, and breathing hole.
introduces: front glass panel, ink level, grip band, and nib details
requires_prior_elements: faceted bottle contour and fountain-pen silhouette

frames.visible_job: Adds a few inner glass edges and reserved pale reflection shapes to the existing bottle without filling or lettering the front panel.
introduces: glass edge lines and reserved highlight shapes
requires_prior_elements: faceted bottle contour and fountain-pen silhouette
front glass panel, ink level, grip band, and nib details

frames.visible_job: Adds blue-black pencil to the established ink area, warm brown to the barrel, pale metal tone to the nib, and one soft shadow beneath both objects.
introduces: blue-black ink, warm brown barrel, metal nib tone, and cast shadow
requires_prior_elements: faceted bottle contour and fountain-pen silhouette
front glass panel, ink level, grip band, and nib details
glass edge lines and reserved highlight shapes

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens established glass, pen, nib, ink, reflection, color, and shadow marks without adding a new object or label.

rejection_checks: No bottle plane, neck, ink line, front panel, pen contour, grip band, nib detail, highlight, color area, or shadow may first appear in the final panel.
The bottle and pen must keep one arrangement, angle, scale, and crop throughout all six panels.
The bottle front panel must remain blank, with no letters, logo, label text, numbers, or fake signature.
The finish must look like a handmade pencil still life rather than polished product art or vector stationery clip art.
No cap, paper sheet, handwriting, hand, desk clutter, or extra stationery may appear.
